frame_label: The Makeup Paradox: Unnatural Yet Expected
generated_comment: Aiyo, these girls now put so much makeup and filter also, then in real life they look totally different. Better to be neat and natural only, otherwise people will think she is trying too hard for attention. But at the same time, a woman should also maintain herself properly, not come looking dull and careless.
label: misogynistic
annotation_note: This is misogynistic because it places women in a double bind: criticizing visible makeup as deceptive and attention-seeking while also expecting women to maintain an attractive appearance. It encodes the 'natural beauty' preference, deception framing, and policing of female grooming.
original_frame: Women's Safety as Her Own Responsibility
persona_text: You are Meena. Age 54. Married. A homemaker in Chennai. Your daughter in law Kavitha has just been offered a job that requires her to travel by local train and walk 10 minutes from the station to the office every day. She is excited about the job. You are sitting with your son and Kavitha at dinner after she shared the news. You are giving your opinion on the job and specifically on the commute and what can happen to women who travel alone and whether the salary is worth the risk.
